reviews.review_msg: I do not recommend his classes

Definitely a challenging course, but so worth it! The material is fascinating and really makes you think. The exams test your understanding with tricky application questions that cover multiple topics at once. If you're ready for a challenge and enjoy learning deeply, this class is for you! :)

Professor Dheva is amazing! The course is super engaging and covers interesting topics, though there's a lot to remember. While the exams were definitely challenging (but in a good way!), the way Professor Dheva blends multiple topics into application-based questions really helps deepen your understanding. Highly recommend if you enjoy a challenge!
That one review might be fake tbh
[MBB 322] 2nd midterm average was around 33%, but they changed it to 54% with "lenient marking." Take that as you will.

The course is pretty interesting but so many things to remember. Dheva mostly asks application based question that based on multiple topic rather than just one topic. Exams were difficult. But pretty interesting stuff.

Course: MBB 324 (idk why I can't select or add this course on RMP) Slides are so messy and random. Midterms were fair but still hard. Final exam has 2 parts; both insanely hard. Please never take this guy. GPA definitely took a hit.

He's a nice guy, and he presents content in memorable/engaging ways. He'll ask people for answers in class, but doesn't judge responses. Exams are fair (study the objective lists & reference sheets), but MBB321 is a tough memorization course. I can see how others think his slides are a lot, but tbh my ADHD brain would make them the same way.

Dr Young has been such a pleasure this semester! Teaches with lots of passion and a great sense of humour. Lectures were taught in person and were recorded as well, which made learning so accessible! Lots of opportunities to make up for lost marks, and the timing for exams was extremely fair imo. Tip: review learning objectives for exam prep!

Dr. Young is one of the best MBB profs there are. Teaches material clearly, fair exams, nice marking scheme, reviews topics often to help students. 321 with Dr. Young made it much easier to understand all the pathways because of his teaching style. Would 100% take another course with him

Unnecessarily complicates lectures by going into too much depth. Very messy slides filled with information, and doesn't give enough time for tests. MTs which are supposed to be 50 min are 45 minutes, and he started distributing exams after class starts. Also, some questions are out of the syllabus.

Literally, take any other professor but him.

Truth-not an easy course but def fun to learn. keep up with the lect* it accumulates fast. I studied Obj for exams, I recommend having obj and lect side by side when you study. Exams were fair but get ur timing right(min/q). Dr. Y an awe prof. his teaching was very logical, metaphoric, humorous.

Enthusiastic prof but does not present information in a clear and concise way. As previous readers have suggested, better to self-learn. Did not enjoy this course, material relatively easy as there are lots of resources online. His notes? Not good, crowded, and hard to navigate.

enthusiastic lecturer but holy crap UGLIEST and most cluttered slides ever, makes concepts unnecessarily difficult to understand. better off self-teaching and watching youtube videos tbh. learning objectives somewhat helpful for tests but they are also cluttered and hard to interpret at times.

Lecture slides are disorganized with lots of clutter and every week there is an objective list filled with LOTS of info on what is examinable(also cluttered). Exams are ok, I had ingrid for 222 and wish I took her for 321 instead. Edgar takes feedback though and changed how the canvas was organized but also is adamant on not posting tutorial slides

Was a pleasure having Dr Young teach this course. Made attending morning lectures interesting and he was always very welcoming. The grading system was student friendly, consisting of 6 exams (50 pts each), but total exam grade was only worth 250 points. Also 7 quizzes but only graded best 5. Cumulative final (last 2 exams) were pretty tough

Had quizzes every week and exams when we didn't. 6 exams worth 50 marks each for a total of 300 but only total calculated using 250. 7 quizzes worth 10 marks each for a total of 60 but only calculated using 50. Exams worth 90%, quizzes worth 10%. Hard Cumulative final exam (2 exams with a 30 minute gap)
He is a wonderful prof. Have Iclicker to make students go to the morning class which is good. Keep up with the review sheets.

He is an okay prof, but overhead notes written are messy and hard to read. Sometimes goes on a tangent and often you will have to go over slides that he did not present in class. Tries a bit hard to be funny. Lots of iClicker questions for credit. He gives out review lists that the exams are based on and has a fair grading system.

I thought he is a very nice and funny teacher and genuinely cares about his students doing well. Clickers almost every class for participation, gives out "cheat sheets" to follow along, slides aren't too bad, tests are very fair. Uploads his slides and whatever he writes on the overhead after every class so even if you skip a day you can catch up.
She gives ASMR inaudible lectures.

Dr Northwood, has made courses that students often fear MBB 321easy. She teaches as if students are little babies helps u understand deeper. She gives hints as to what is examinable and what is not. To study for her Exams, i highly recommend the past exams she has posted, problem sets, and past tutorial questions. the mcqs have very close answers

I really enjoyed Dr. Northwood's lectures and personality. What I found frustrating was her grading scale. I underestimated the first exam and failed it. Got my act together and did really well on every other exam, however with sub 60% being a fail, 60-70% being a D, 70-80% being a C it was impossible to bring my average up to a B or A.

very test heavy, boring and monotonous and wants memorization. finals was 2 exams, cumulative and the end chapters, what even? very slow so just wants to get over by telling whats on the exam so ots very individual study.

As a non MBB major, Dr. Northwood is the sole reason I took this course. I absolutely love her. She tests on small things but is very clear about exactly what it will be, making a pretty content heavy course manageable. Achieve is optional, the textbook solutions Northwood posts (for free) are the same content if you don't want to pay.

Dr. Northwood is an amazing professor. If you haven't taken one of her classes yet, I highly recommend it. I've had her for both 231 and 321 and if I could take another I would. She's super transparent on what will be on exams and what to prepare for. If you study what she asks you to, it's pretty hard to do poorly in her class.
Awesome professor. Just follow her lectures and practice questions on Achieve.

MBB 321 is a difficult class, and Dr. Northwood's ability to be clear on what will be tested is a godsend in that regard. The concepts are difficult to grasp, but she makes sure that she is very clear on what she needs you to know. Her practice questions on Achieve and the Canvas Lecture quizzes are usually word for word copied as exam questions.

Dr. Northwood's class was a blast! She lets you know EXACTLY what's on the tests; review sessions and tutorials are super helpful. Lecture slides can be a bit dense at times but she does a great job at clearing up concepts. Tests are extremely fair (just follow the slides + tutorials and you're set)

Great lecturer. Makes it EXTREMELY clear what's on the tests (will straight up go through her lecture slides and be like "this is on the test, this isn't, this isn't, oh this is super important, etc, etc." Very pleasant speaker and seems to care about students' success. Always smiley and seems to enjoy her work!
She is probably one of the best profs I have ever had and I would recommend taking any mbb class where she is teaching !!

Lecture Material was quite interesting. However, the learning objectives and exam materials can sometimes be inconsistent. Exams were not bad tho. When asked with certain questions she commonly refers to the textbook. Her review sessions were ok at best although not the most efficient. She is very nice and quite passionate about teaching.

Dr. Northwood is literally the best! She makes MBB as fun as possible despite it being a hard course. Her exams are so easy if you just study her slides and are always so straightforward. No trick questions whatsoever and tells you exactly what she expects. Definitely recommend her for every MBB class!

If Dr. Northwood taught any class, I would seriously be tempted to take it. She is amazing. Her lectures are clear and concise. I have perhaps in my long years at SFU never had a professor who was more clear on what they wanted you to know. She may have many exams, but it is because she wants to make sure you really understand the material

Currently taking my 3rd course with Dr. Northwood. Made it my goal to take as many courses as possible with her. She brightens my spirits every time I enter her class. An MBB degree isn't complete until you've taken a class with this LEGEND. Her selfies with her cat, cat stories (disasters), and cat memes (that she makes herself) are hilarious

Dr. Northwood makes lectures more bearable with her comedic approach to teaching. Never felt like a chore to attend, although she came late a lot. She gives out lots of hints, and the tests are predictable. Not cumulative which is a life-saver. Be diligient and study what she tells you, and I'm fairly sure you'll get at least a B.

Just show up to class to note what she says is on the exam; she gives these hints every lecture and will make you're life easy. Lastly, study the learning outcomes of each lecture and absolutely do the textbook questions and the other questions within her notes because these show up on the exam! I just told you how to get an A. Thank me later.

She's a crystal clear prof. No tricks. She's awesome at explaining things but sometimes I felt like the notes on the slides weren't enough so I would highly recommend not missing lectures. The course became exhausting after awhile because of 5 exams. Also, memorization will only take you so far. Work hard, results will show. .
pretty impressive lectures, study materials are pretty straightforward, you will get a good grade for this class if u have done the assigned questions from her.
Not the best prof for learning the content

Astounding prof! One of my top favorites, she truly exemplifies her passion for her career and the biology of the human body,  everyday, eveyclass. Make sure you take at least one course with her, she only teaches 321 though (she should teach O-chem and all the MBB pre-req's) Go to class!!!

I know people have mixed opinions about Dr. Northwood, but I absolutely LOVE HER. She made MBB 321 a wonderful adventure. She chooses the best TAs for her courses, and is very clear on what to know for exams. All exams were fair and If you attend tutorials, they will boost your mark.

Dr. Northwood was one of my favourite professors in university. She gave great lectures, and there was a lot of back and forth banter with students. We even pranked her with the entire classes' phone alarms. She took it well.  She tells you what to study.  My study group of friends all got A's or A+'s. Half of us are in med school or PhD programs.

Dr. Northwood is hilarious. Lectures are super interesting, though it's hard to absorb the material the first time she goes through it. Lots of textbook questions are re-used on the exam, and she tells you exactly what to focus on at the end of her lectures. Can come across as a bit mean, but is willing to go over important concepts before exams.

Dr. Northwood is a great professor! Her lectures were really interesting, although she is a bit intimidating at first.  Don't skip class because she tells you exactly what is going to be on the test. Also, she puts questions from the textbook and practice exams on the test, which is nice.
Worst teacher ever. I dont even want to talk about this. Shes just terrible. There are hundreds of great teachers out there who would do a better job. She should not be teaching this course.
Shes the toughest grader Ive ever had. Her grading scale is insanely tough. Its tough to pass her course. I wish I never had to take it.
I thought Dr. Northwood was okay but not a very nice prof by any means. very unapproachable and extremely moody. If possible, I would avoid her course in the future.
one of the worst prof I've ever had. avoid her course if possible.

I went into this class absolutely hating MBB. I came out of this class absolutely loving it, that's how great Dr. Northwood is. She is by far my favourite prof so far in University. Her lectures are extremely interesting and the best part of all-she tells you EXACTLY what is going to be on the exams. Honestly, how can you not succeed? I got an A+!

Our final was on the last day of class. Her lectures are fast but she gets the point across. She tells you whats on the exam, and the exams overall were somewhat fair, just watch out for tricky questions. Make sure you do the given book questions and the learning objectives at the end of each lecture. Her grading scale is very harsh.

I have no clue how she is still allowed to teach. She doesn't know how to teach and i think that is reflected in the horrible class averages for all three midterms. Just make sure to do the assigned questions and her objectives at the end of each lecture.

this prof should be sent to where the sun dont shine, we give her card and prank and the students get stabbed in the back.  i wish i had taken this course with davidson now. her grading was totally unfair and scale was even worse... 80% is a B+/B... are u kidding me? We didnt even have enough time to study for finals. just utterly horrible period.

She is definitely a GREAT prof. You can't possibly fall asleep in her class for she goes through thirty or so slides in one class hour, but  this doesn't mean her lectures are too much. She covers important materials thoroghly and she even tells us what will be on the test. She is fair. I would strongly recommend her.

This woman is brilliant in her field of work. Her lectures make that very obvious. Having said that, she's also incredibly disrespectful and condescending to some students. Whether you fall on her good or bad side is arbitrary.

LOVE HER! one of the best teachers I have ever had. Yes she can be rude and she ain't no walk in the park but shes soooooo good at teaching. You could listen to her for hours and she makes it super clear! Would love to take another class with her!
Make sure you do recommended problems and practice questions for the exam. Also attend lectures because she tells you what topics are important for the exam.
expects waaaaayyy tooo much from her students and when u ask her for help or study advice she is very very rude!

I thought she was scary after she yelled at our class for bombing the 1st midterm (it was hard!), but when I went to her office hours she was *so* nice that I completely changed my mind. She is a little scatterbrained sometimes, but seriously...teaching and being the mbb undergrad academic advisor is a lot of work.

very moody, kinda scary sometimes! she tells you what will be on the exam during lecture since the level of material is very high! i personally dont like her wording on exams; i find it confusing. remember, with her " you will get whatever you will get!" dont rely too much on the curve. above 86%-->A.
No Comments

OMG!!! She's ok for 322 but she is HECTIC for 321 (on top of the already super-heavy-and-sheer-load of material killer class) Goes over 30 something slides a lecture, non-stop! She can't even breathe herself. Hard exam that screwed a lot of people over (as opposed to the high average in mbb322)Don't have time to get down anything she says!
She is bad only if you are stupid
Can't really get some help from her regarding the course material. she usually not avaliable for extra help.

Dr. Northwood is excellent in terms of teaching, clarity, attitude, personality, etc.  As for going to her during non-office hours, would you want a few hundred MBB undergrads popping by whenever they felt like it?

Very clear on what you need to know and what you don't, tests memorization and context at the same time.  As long as you go IN office hours she is really nice (but will bite you if you are not in office hours) Overall great teacher

scatter brain.  she cant maintain a clear line of thinking.  she formates her course so that you memorise certain details but never really learn anything.  for learning she is terrible, for getting an easy B she's ok.

her focus is not to teach principles of metabolism but to teach you to memorize a rediculous amount of detail.  if you like to memorize she is for you but if you actually want to learn something at universty then forget it.
Dr. Northwood rules!!  Makes metabolism about as interesting as it can be made.  If she says it's on the exam, it is, and if she says it's not, then it's not.  The tests can be a bit long, though.
She gives good notes, but the exams are not fair
REALLY NICE!!!
She talks so fast - it drives me crazy!  But she is apparently the best in the department for MBB teachers!
Great teaching, tough exam

Worth taking a course with Dr. Williamson. He will sincerely tell you what to focus on with no BS. If you do bad on any of the midterms and great on the final. He will take your final exam's mark as the overall. Study from lecture notes. Not everything will be tested on. But when he says "this is VERY important" its for a reason he is saying it!

Dr Davidson is a good prof. It's true that he reads out straight from his lecture notes but MBB321 is an introductory course, so there is not much to add to it although he adds lots of medicine, and pharmaceutical related concepts to course content. He tries hard to be funny :D He tells what he wants from you.  I will take more courses with him.

Come to class and ace MT 1 cause he tells you what's important so listen. Study hard and youll do well despite MT 2. I liked his jokes. Very friendly and good additional info. If you NEED the course split into 5 midterms then take with Northwood but Dr Davidson was a great professor anyway despite compounding.

ATTEND CLASS. He will literally point out what will be on the exam, however this is an MBB class and it is 321 so the after the first exam it can be a lot of info to remember. Very nice person and is always there to help. I skipped some classes because lectures are recorded, but try not to miss too many because he'll talk at the end with a mute mic

Overall the class is pretty tough, there is a lot of material to memorize. He does give a lot of helpful hints during lectures as to what he wants you to know. Tests get increasingly more difficult, the final is very tough... I would definitely recommend him though, makes 321 a lot easier to understand compared to other profs who teach the class.

Pretty good prof but 4.3/5 is probably a little generous in my opinion. I agree that this courses might have double the material of a standard MBB course. Dr. Davidson does a great job of providing hints for exams. Review classes were extremely helpful at end of semester, lots of questions on final were taken directly from there. 8:30am slot brutal
HE IS ABSOLUTELY AMAZING, AND HELPFUL, MUST TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM!!

The course itself is A LOT of work but it is not impossible to do well.  Two mt's each worth 20% and final can be made to be worth 60, 80 or 100% of your final grade if it works in your favor. I loved the class and did very well. He tells u exactly what to expect on tests and drops a lot of hints.  Its an MBB course, no MBB course is an easy ride

An awesome prof. Was clear about everything that we were going to need to know for the exams. Exams were not easy, but if you took his headsups seriously, there was no reason not to do well. Plus, he made a topic that would have been dry actually quite interesting. If you have to take it, take it with him. You won't regret it.

He was possibly the nicest prof at SFU. His exams were not easy but he did tell us what was expected. A lots of material! If you can't avoid Mbb 321, take it with Dr. Davidson. It will make your life lots easier

Straight forward, tells you exactly what's on the exam, always available for office hour or e-mail questions. The course itself is hard but he makes it digestible. Midterms coast you in, final was brutal. Keep up with this course!

He started off telling us exactly what was going to be on the first midterm, which was great. Then, the second midterm hits, with an average of 47%. The final was even more difficult, as he expected us to know little things he covered in passing. If you did well on the first midterm, you were set.
organic chem final was a piece of cake compared to his final, you basically have to know everything.

Final was BRUTAL, the course was already overwhelming and he expects so much detail from u! True that he does tell u whats "on" the exam but he adds on additional questions that will drive u crazy. So make sure u do good on first MT as his exams get more difficult and much longer. He is BIG on enzyme fates, there was A LOT of them in the final.
Awsome prof, by far one of the best I have ever had. he tells you exactly what will be on his exam. He makes a challenging course very easy and interesting..

If you are good at memorizing reaction pathways, charts, graphs and other stuff this is the class for you and you will like his exams. Oh and the powerpoint notes are really really good if you want to dig into the material.
The best prof I've had at SFU.  He gives you a real appreciation and understanding of metabolism

The material is overwhelming, but he really points out the take-home message. He literally tells you what will be on the exam. He's a really good prof in that he makes students not only memorize the material, but really understand it inside out. Very helpful, funny, and nice.

The amount for this course is enormous, could double most of the existing courses~ The prof expects a lot from you! He wishes to have a 99% avg while in reality, it stands around 50~60%. He is a professional, but this is why he lacks understanding on students' perspectives

AWESOME GUY. hes one of those profs that is actually there for the students and will go out of his way to help you (hilarious too) a rare good prof! kinda reminds me of sean connery. His exams are challenging but fair just A LOT OF WRITING! i highly recomened Willie!

Funny guy.  Tells what will be on exams, but don't rely only on that b/c sometimes stuff he said wasn't on exam/sometimes it was.  Midterms and final had mostly fair questions (with exception of a few), but very long (use time wisely)...non-stop writing, little thinking time.
he tells u what he wants u to know for the exam.."no surprises"...a lot of memorization..though he makes one of the hardcore mbb courses not as tough
His exams were'nt too bad, challenging yet fair.  Don't memorize too much details for this class - just the 'big picture'.

Dr. Chin was a decent prof. Her exams are very specific and require recalling random facts. Aside from the unnecessary difficulty of exams she is actually really good at explaining difficult concepts and makes herself accessible outside of class help over email in her own time. Attend class as she gives hints as to what will be on the exam.

This was Dr. Chin's first time teaching MBB 321, and I think she did it very well. She drops LOTS of hints for the exams during lecture. She makes it very clear what you need to do to do well on the exam.
